channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Best AI Agent Governance Tools in 2026: A Layer-by-Layer Guide
item.description: The best AI agent governance tool for a given team depends on which layer of the problem they need to control: agent identity, runtime action validation, model-level guardrails, observability, or...

item.title: The First Microprocessor Was the Intel 4004
item.description: Every connected device you build today, from an ESP32 blinking on a breadboard to an industrial gateway pushing sensor data to the cloud, is a descendant of one small chip released in 1971: the...

item.title: What I learned building an AI video background changer
item.description: Hey DEV community, I recently launched bgchanger.video, an AI video background changer for removing or replacing video backgrounds directly in the browser. The idea is simple: many creators, indie...

item.title: AI Agent Runtime Policy: Stop Dangerous Tool Calls Before They Execute
item.description: An AI agent does not need to be malicious to damage production. It only needs the wrong tool, the wrong database, the wrong customer ID, or one confident step that nobody checked. That is the...

item.title: A No-Downgrade Self-Test for GLM-5.2 Coding Routes
item.description: When I route coding work to a lower-cost model, I do not want the first question to be "is it cheaper?" The first question is: Can I tell whether this route behaves like the model I intended to...

item.title: I Built a Personal Paper Engine to Stop Losing Research Papers
item.description: Disclosure: I contribute to the OpenNomos ecosystem where Paper List is listed. I read about 30 research papers a month. Not because I'm an academic—I'm a product person who needs to stay on top...

item.title: The One-Click Exporter: AI Studio Antigravity, Probed to Its Limits
item.description: What nobody tells you about exporting your multi-agent prototype to a local workspace. Every architect who's prototyped a multi-agent app in Google AI Studio eventually hits the same wall: the...

item.title: Hitting the Iceberg REST Catalog Directly: Understanding the Differences Between Glue Data Catalog and S3 Tables
item.description: Original Japanese article: Iceberg REST Catalogを直接叩いて、Glue Data CatalogとS3 Tablesの違いを理解する Introduction I'm Aki, an AWS Community Builder (@jitepengin). Most of the time, when working with Iceberg...

item.title: Unlocking Startup Savings: The Idle-Resource Audit
item.description: Key takeaways Idle resources can account for up to 30% of cloud bills. Proactive audits can prevent silent cost overruns. Real-time monitoring tools can pinpoint resource inefficiencies....

item.title: Semantic Drift in LLMs: How Archetypal Attractors (Like “Goblin”) Emerge and How Structured Reflection Reduces Them
item.description: Large language models often develop recurring symbolic patterns — archetypes, metaphors, and memetic shortcuts — that appear across unrelated contexts. One observed example is the repeated...

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
item.description: Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon...

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
item.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company...

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
item.description: OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X. The news follows Simo's original announcement in...

item.title: OpenAI’s CEO of AGI Deployment, Fidji Simo, Is Stepping Down
item.description: The move comes after Simo took significant medical leave. She will stay on as a part-time adviser.

item.title: Netflix reportedly considers adding always-on channels
item.description: Netflix is thinking about adding always-on channels that would stream specific shows and movies, according to The Wall Street Journal. The move sounds like a Netflix version of always-on services...

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

item.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
item.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

item.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
item.description: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

item.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
item.description: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

item.title: Patch for Windows Defender 0-day could allow attackers to fill hard disk
item.description: The feud between NightmareEclipse and Microsoft shows no signs of resolving soon.

item.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
item.description: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave...

item.title: The floatable, powerful Soundcore Boom 2 speaker is over half off
item.description: Bluetooth speakers with big sound and great features are hard to find for under $100, with most offerings being some variation of the same basic (and often small) design. Thankfully, through July...

item.title: Allstate accuses Broadcom of auditing it because it quit VMware, CA
item.description: Broadcom accuses Allstate of dodging VMware audits.

item.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What...

item.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
item.description: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update,...

item.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
item.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI...

item.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description: Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API....

item.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as...
